content.question: What is the marginal causal effect of the 6- and 8-year track structure itself (separate from admissions selectivity and school reputation effects) on segregation and inequality outcomes?
content.reduction_path: Commission a matched-cohort quasi-experimental study comparing otherwise similar students in tracked vs. comprehensive intake systems, controlling for residential location and primary-school reputation; or conduct a regional pilot in which track admissions are modified while school-quality measures are held constant, with before-after outcome tracking
